summary: DHH reflects on the concept of borrowing against the future, using bank credit as a metaphor. He expresses mixed feelings—awe and disbelief—toward people disciplined enough to avoid credit entirely. He questions whether avoiding temptation is superior to facing and managing it. The brief post ends with a philosophical question about self-trust.
key_points: There's a tension between avoiding temptation entirely and having the discipline to handle it responsibly
Forgoing credit completely may reflect a lack of self-trust rather than pure discipline
The ability to use borrowed resources wisely is its own form of strength
money_quotes: I've always been part in awe, part in disbelief of people with enough self-control to forgo opening a bank credit.
I'm trying to imagine having the strength to avoid temptation, but not to handle the temptation itself — despite the times of benefit.
How much do you trust yourself?
key_insight: True discipline may not be avoiding temptation altogether, but trusting yourself enough to handle it responsibly.
